title: Sine Function Visualization
description: Interactive plot of the sine function with slider control to explore points along the curve. Demonstrates periodicity, amplitude, and relationship to the unit circle.
creator: Claude Skills - Math Function Plotter
subject: mathematics
trigonometry
precalculus
calculus
visualization
interactive learning
learningObjectives: Calculate the value of sin(x) for any x in radians by moving the slider and reading the red point's coordinates on the curve
Explain how the sine function's amplitude of 1 and period of 2π appear in the plotted curve over the domain [-2π, 2π]
Identify the x-values where sin(x) reaches its maximum, its minimum, and its zero crossings
Relate the sine function's output to the vertical component of a point moving around the unit circle
Predict the y-value of the point before moving the slider to a chosen x-coordinate, then verify with the hover tooltip
